---
title: "SEC_R001: No Hardcoded Credentials"
subtitle: "Absolute prohibition of credentials in source code"
chapter: "CH07"
category: "rule"
number: "SEC_R001"
date-created: "2025-08-30"
date-modified: "2025-08-30"
author: "Principle Revisor"
type: "security-rule"
parent-principle: "MP110"
enforcement: "MANDATORY"
severity: "CRITICAL"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
---

## Rule Statement

**NO credentials, passwords, API keys, tokens, or secrets of any kind may be hardcoded in source files.**

## Implementation Requirements

### Prohibited Patterns

```r
# VIOLATION - Never do any of these:
password <- "my_password"
api_key <- "sk-proj-1234567890"
db_password <- "postgres123"
secret_token <- base64encode("my_secret")

# VIOLATION - Even in comments:
# old_key <- "sk-proj-old-key"  # DO NOT COMMIT

# VIOLATION - Configuration files:
config <- list(
  password = "actual_password"  # NEVER
)
```

### Required Patterns

```r
# CORRECT - Environment variables only:
password <- Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD")
api_key <- Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")

# CORRECT - With validation:
get_credential <- function(var_name) {
  value <- Sys.getenv(var_name)
  if (nchar(value) == 0) {
    stop(sprintf("Environment variable %s not set", var_name))
  }
  return(value)
}

db_password <- get_credential("DB_PASSWORD")
```

## Detection Methods

### Regex Patterns for Scanning

```regex
# API Keys
sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{48}
api[_-]?key\s*=\s*["'][^"']+["']

# Passwords
password\s*=\s*["'][^"']+["']
pwd\s*=\s*["'][^"']+["']
pass\s*=\s*["'][^"']+["']

# Tokens
token\s*=\s*["'][^"']+["']
bearer\s+[a-zA-Z0-9\-._~+/]+=*

# AWS Keys
AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}
```

## Enforcement

- Pre-commit hooks MUST scan for violations
- CI/CD pipelines MUST reject commits with credentials
- Code reviews MUST check for credential patterns
- Regular security audits MUST scan entire codebase

## Remediation

If a credential is found:
1. Immediately revoke the exposed credential
2. Remove from all branches and history
3. Generate new credentials
4. Update all systems using the credential
5. Document the incident

## Related Rules

- SEC_R002: Environment Variable Standards
- SEC_R003: Credential Rotation Requirements
- SEC_R004: Secret Storage Patterns
